title: Evangelions TV series music feels too repetitive
display_title: Evangelions TV series music feels too repetitive
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion's TV series' music feels too repetitive             Quote Post by eva14 » Mon Oct 27, 2014 11:48 am Let me put this out firstly; I love the soundtrack of Neon Genesis Evangelion but each and every episode just reuse the same BGMs over and over again, and while this hasn't annoyed me to the point of stop watching I wish they would have had variety.

[]: Re: Evangelion's TV series' music feels too repetitive             Quote Post by Dataprime » Mon Oct 27, 2014 1:41 pm eva14 wrote: each and every episode just reuse the same BGMs over and over again

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Oct 27, 2014 1:59 pm Actually, as far as anime soundtracks go at the time, NGE had more original music per episode than most. Every episode introduced a new musical theme somewhere. Sure, the rest of it is repetitive, but Shiro Sagisu at least went that extra mile most other composers didn’t by including a new piece with every episode.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Mon Oct 27, 2014 2:14 pm Did you know "decisive battle" was actually inspired by a score from James Bonds Goldfinger?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Oct 27, 2014 3:53 pm That's how a lot of anime works; you'll have several select themes that you break out every now and then when there's an event of a specific kind happening. At least the music was nice, as you mentioned.

[]:             Quote Post by eva14 » Mon Oct 27, 2014 6:28 pm Ray wrote: Did you know "decisive battle" was actually inspired by a score from James Bonds Goldfinger?

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Mon Oct 27, 2014 6:58 pm Try watching Twin Peaks. Believe me, there's a series who's music gets repetitive. You end up hearing the Laura Palmer theme no less than 4 times in each episode 'cause David Lynch gets a boner every time he hears it. I think in terms of the music of Eva, I did notice several tracks that were used repeatedly (Decisive Battle and Spending Time in Preparation being the chief offenders), but nevertheless, they never felt over used. I do think that Anno should have tried to use more of the tracks that were ultimately rejected, or saw little to no use. I think the biggest change I would have made personally would be to swap out Asuka's "hick girl" theme for A-2, which was originally slated to be her theme.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Oct 27, 2014 9:46 pm That hokey track is the worst in the show, so it's appropriate.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Mon Oct 27, 2014 10:06 pm You mean the banjo song that plays during the funny moments right?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Oct 28, 2014 2:03 am If there's a problem with the BGM, it's that it feels rather cheesy when it actually intrudes on the consciousness; with the classical pieces being so abruptly different from the rest as to feel somewhat out of place.

[]:             Quote Post by Sgt. Griff » Tue Oct 28, 2014 3:23 am At least it isn't Kill La Kill, the music in that is repeated so often that it becomes entirely comic, even the sad Ad Lib piece.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Oct 28, 2014 3:25 am Honestly, I love the way that KLK handled their OST. It got to the point that you could hear the opening strains of a theme and start shivering in anticipation of the ultimate badassery that was to come-- or the heartbreak. I adored it, personally.
